hypothesis: If a CNN (e.g., EfficientNet-B0) is first pretrained on data-rich additive-free NCM SEM images and then fine-tuned on a small additive-induced SEM dataset, then it will achieve higher accuracy/F1 in classifying NCM composition and aging state than models trained from scratch or without source-domain pretraining because transferable morphological features (e.g., cracks and surface boundaries) are shared across the two domains.
